steps.action: Inspect BreakdownTab.tsx source code for Framer Motion imports
steps.expected: Framer Motion motion and AnimatePresence imported
cardVariants with hidden/visible/exit states and stagger delay based on index
steps.observed: cardVariants at line ~163: hidden={opacity:0, y:20}, visible uses custom index with delay=index*0.1s, duration=0.4s, cubic-bezier easing [0.25,0.1,0.25,1.0], exit={opacity:0, y:-10}

steps.action: Verify SceneCard uses motion.div with stagger variants
steps.expected: motion.div with custom={index}, variants={cardVariants}, initial='hidden', animate='visible'
steps.observed: SceneCard component at line ~182 renders <motion.div layout custom={index} variants={cardVariants} initial='hidden' animate='visible' exit='exit'>

steps.action: Verify AnimatePresence wraps scene card list
steps.expected: AnimatePresence wrapping the scene cards map
steps.observed: AnimatePresence mode='popLayout' wraps fetchedScenes.map() rendering SceneCard components

steps.action: Verify Add button also has staggered delay
steps.observed: Add New Scene button uses motion.div with delay: fetchedScenes.length * 0.1 + 0.3, creating smooth sequential appearance after all cards

steps.action: Navigate to Breakdown tab in browser and verify rendering
steps.observed: Breakdown tab displays correctly with empty state message, no console errors

codeInspection.sceneCardMotionDiv: <motion.div layout custom={index} variants={cardVariants} initial='hidden' animate='visible' exit='exit'>

assertions.title: Breakdown Backend Uses Structured JSON Prompts

steps.action: Inspect generate-storylines/prompts.ts for structured JSON prompt construction
steps.expected: Prompts built using structured JSON objects with explicit field mapping, not string concatenation
steps.observed: getStorylineUserPrompt() builds projectContextJson and sceneRequirements as structured JS objects, serialized with JSON.stringify(generationRequest, null, 2)

steps.action: Verify scene generation requirements include explicit fields for scene count
Verify shot types are explicitly defined
Verify locations field is required per scene
steps.expected: location in required fields per scene

steps.action: Verify characters per scene implicit via shot fields
steps.observed: requiredFieldsPerShot = ['shot_type', 'description', 'visual_prompt', 'camera_movement', 'duration_seconds', 'composition_notes']; description field captures character involvement per shot

steps.action: Verify gemini-schemas.ts enforces structured JSON output schema
steps.expected: JSON schema with scene_breakdown array including shot_ideas
steps.observed: STORYLINE_RESPONSE_SCHEMA defines scene_breakdown array with required fields [scene_number, title, description, location, shot_ideas], shot_ideas has minItems:3, maxItems:6 with required [shot_type, description, visual_prompt, camera_movement, duration_seconds]

steps.action: Verify format-specific briefs use structured JSON objects
steps.expected: Format briefs (commercial, music_video, infotainment, short_film) as structured objects
steps.observed: Switch statement builds formatBrief as typed objects for each format: commercial has product/brandName/targetAudience/mainMessage/callToAction/tone/adDuration/platform/brandGuidelines; music_video has artistName/trackTitle/genre/bpm/performanceRatio; infotainment has topic/targetAudience/keyFacts/educationalGoals/hostStyle/visualStyle; short_film has genre/tone/duration/visualStyle

steps.action: Verify index.ts passes structured prompts to generation endpoint
steps.expected: Generation call uses structured prompt from prompts.ts
steps.observed: index.ts calls getStorylineUserPrompt(project, generate_alternative, existingStorylines) and passes result to gemini-storyline-generation endpoint with responseSchema from gemini-schemas.ts

codeInspection.structuredPromptMethod: getStorylineUserPrompt() - builds projectContextJson + sceneRequirements as JS objects, serialized via JSON.stringify

summary: Tested 2 assertions: 2 passed. VAL-SETUP-013 confirmed via code inspection that BreakdownTab.tsx implements Framer Motion staggered fade-up animations with cardVariants (hidden→visible with index*0.1s delay) wrapped in AnimatePresence. VAL-SETUP-014 confirmed via code inspection that generate-storylines edge function uses structured JSON prompts with explicit fields for sceneCount, shotTypes, locations, requiredFieldsPerScene/Shot, and format-specific briefs — all serialized via JSON.stringify, not string concatenation.
